meta.purpose: Encode performance judgment for smooth, battery-aware, accessible web animation.
meta.load_condition: Load when the task involves web animation that may jank, stutter, drain battery, lag on scroll, or need performance profiling.

axioms.one_sentence: Prefer transform and opacity for continuous animation before any layout-triggering property.
axioms.full_statement: Prefer transform and opacity for continuous animation before any layout-triggering property.
axioms.why: Browsers can composite transform and opacity entirely on the GPU without triggering layout, paint, or style recalculation, which is the only reliable path to 60fps on low-end devices.
applies_when: Continuous UI animations like loading spinners or modal transitions
Micro-interactions triggered by user hover or click events
Scroll-linked visual effects on mid-to-low-end mobile devices
does_not_apply_when: Opening dropdown menus or accordions that require document flow changes
Complex SVG path morphing or shape extrusions that cannot be expressed with matrix transforms
Initial page load content reflow where layout shifting is intentionally used for progressive disclosure
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will generate GPU-composited animations that still cause severe frame drops because it blindly ignores paint-heavy properties or forces layout thrashing in synchronous callbacks.

axioms.one_sentence: Always profile before optimizing. Never assume you know the bottleneck.
axioms.full_statement: Always profile before optimizing. Never assume you know the bottleneck.
axioms.why: Common agent failure mode: guessing 'too many elements' when the real cause is forced synchronous layout, repaint storms, or scroll handler thrashing.
applies_when: Debugging janky scroll performance in a data-heavy dashboard
Investigating unexplained frame drops after a recent framework upgrade
Optimizing a web application reported to lag specifically on Android Chrome
does_not_apply_when: Adding a standard debounce to a known scroll event listener in a disposable prototype
Applying a CSS will-change hint to a single fixed-position navigation bar
Writing initial boilerplate for a static marketing landing page with zero interactive elements
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will waste development cycles rewriting rendering pipelines or adding unnecessary requestAnimationFrame wrappers while missing the actual bottleneck like unthrottled event handlers or forced synchronous layout.

axioms.one_sentence: Honor prefers-reduced-motion and provide motion-free alternatives for every meaningful animation.
axioms.full_statement: Honor prefers-reduced-motion and provide motion-free alternatives for every meaningful animation.
axioms.why: Excessive motion harms users with vestibular disorders. Accessibility is not optional — animation without a reduced-motion fallback is broken.
applies_when: Implementing hero section parallax or fade-in transitions on marketing sites
Adding notification toasts, success spinners, or state change animations in web apps
Creating interactive data visualizations with animated data transitions
does_not_apply_when: Generating static CSS gradients or background colors that do not animate over time
Writing utility classes for layout spacing, typography, or grid alignment
Building a terminal-style CLI interface rendered in a browser canvas
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will ship unmitigated animations that trigger vestibular disorders or severe nausea and fail WCAG 2.2 accessibility compliance audits.

axioms.one_sentence: Define an explicit frame budget per interaction and reject animations that exceed it.
axioms.full_statement: Define an explicit frame budget per interaction and reject animations that exceed it.
axioms.why: Smoothness is a design quality, not an optimization afterthought. A dropped frame destroys the perception of premium faster than bad color choices.
applies_when: Designing micro-interactions for mobile browsers on mid-tier hardware
Implementing scroll-driven or continuous background animations
Establishing animation guidelines for a shared component library
Reviewing animation code that relies on layout-thrashing properties instead of transforms or opacity
does_not_apply_when: Prototyping early-stage concepts where rapid iteration outweighs frame-perfect precision
Implementing decorative, non-interactive background effects that do not overlap with user input regions
Targeting controlled kiosk or dedicated hardware environments with guaranteed high-end GPUs
Applying prefers-reduced-motion fallbacks that intentionally bypass or disable animation chains entirely
axioms.failure_risk: Enforcing strict frame budgets on decorative elements or early prototypes will waste engineering hours optimizing non-critical visuals and delay shipping without improving perceived performance.

ontology.one_sentence: A CSS property (transform, opacity, filter where hardware-accelerated) that the browser can animate on the compositor thread without triggering layout or paint.
ontology.essence: A CSS property (transform, opacity, filter where hardware-accelerated) that the browser can animate on the compositor thread without triggering layout or paint.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'compositor-only property' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: The pattern of writing a style, reading a layout property, and writing again in the same frame, forcing the browser to recalculate layout multiple times per frame.
ontology.essence: The pattern of writing a style, reading a layout property, and writing again in the same frame, forcing the browser to recalculate layout multiple times per frame.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'layout thrashing' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: The total time available per frame: ~16.7ms at 60fps, shared between script execution, style recalculation, layout, paint, and compositing.
ontology.essence: The total time available per frame: ~16.7ms at 60fps, shared between script execution, style recalculation, layout, paint, and compositing.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'frame budget' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Reading a layout-dependent property immediately after writing a style, forcing the browser to calculate layout synchronously before the next natural frame.
ontology.essence: Reading a layout-dependent property immediately after writing a style, forcing the browser to calculate layout synchronously before the next natural frame.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'forced synchronous layout' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: A CSS property that hints to the browser which properties will animate, allowing it to pre-create compositor layers and GPU textures.
ontology.essence: A CSS property that hints to the browser which properties will animate, allowing it to pre-create compositor layers and GPU textures.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'will-change' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Multiple paint-only changes triggering repeated rasterization within a single frame cycle, exceeding the frame budget.
ontology.essence: Multiple paint-only changes triggering repeated rasterization within a single frame cycle, exceeding the frame budget.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'repaint storm' or describes this scenario.

frameworks.name: Performance diagnosis ladder
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'Performance diagnosis ladder' pattern.
steps: identify whether animation uses compositor-only properties
profile with DevTools Performance panel to find the bottleneck
check for forced synchronous layouts in the animation path
reduce simultaneous animated targets before adding more optimization

frameworks.name: Performance budget ladder
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'Performance budget ladder' pattern.
steps: define the frame budget (e.g., 8ms for animation out of 16.7ms)
if over budget, reduce animated properties, not elements
cache layout reads and batch writes
re-profile and enforce the budget in CI if possible

core_structure.from: animation spec, target device tiers, scroll behavior, layout constraints
core_structure.to: compositor vs layout-triggering property analysis, frame budget fit, accessibility compliance, profiler evidence

core_structure.from: compositor vs layout-triggering property analysis, frame budget fit, accessibility compliance, profiler evidence
core_structure.to: performance diagnosis, optimization strategy, reduced-motion fallback, frame budget report, implementation constraints

stances: If an animation can be expressed with transform and opacity, it should be.
Performance opinions without profiler data are speculation; demand evidence before acting.
